content: ToolBackend resource ownership: the runtime Orchestrator owns and closes the per-run AgentBackend in its finally block; the long-lived ToolBackend is owned by the agent layer (AgentOrchestrator) and closed once at agent shutdown. The ToolBackend ABC carries a default no-op aclose so in-process backends don't have to implement it. Runtime Orchestrator must NOT close injected tools.

description: vigor-core/schemas.py:51 declares Budgets with 5 fields but only max_iterations, max_candidates, max_wall_clock_s are enforced; max_cost_usd and max_tool_retries are paper budgets. VigorError.retryable / RuntimeErrorRecord.retryable are dead metadata; only consumer is vigor-runtime/orchestrator.py:545. No retry loop or backoff exists.
resolution: Strategic-doc VIGOR-ef9a Q5 (cost ceilings on AgentBackend.usage()) and Q8 (wire max_tool_retries into MCPToolBackend.call_tool) close both gaps. Companion ADR drafts will commit to the shapes.

name: domain-adapter-contract-gaps-2026q2
description: DomainAdapter contract generalisation gaps revealed by modality survey (VIGOR-312b §3): G1 IR has no first-class time axis (Manim hides timing inside python_code:str); G2 compile is single-pass (no iterative simulation, no streaming); G3 review consumes one ObservableArtifact at a time (multi-artifact reviewers like 'PDF vs per-slide PNG' or 'narration vs slide' need ad-hoc work); G4 patch is body-level not behaviour-level (no time-relative patch language); G5 RunContext.iteration is a wall-clock counter (must not conflate with media-time position). G1+G4 motivated ADR-0020 advisory convention. G3 motivates a future review_bundle facility (out of scope for picks 1-2).

name: archive-process-lifetime-advisory-lock
description: RunArchive acquires fcntl.flock (POSIX) / msvcrt.locking (Windows) on archive_dir/.archive.lock at __init__ and releases at close(). A process-local refcount registry keyed on resolved root path lets same-process re-opens share the OS lock — required because adapter export() paths construct transient RunArchive(run_dir.parent) on the same root the orchestrator already holds. Per ADR-0035, this is a guardrail against cross-process concurrent writers, not a coordination primitive. weakref.finalize releases on GC as a safety net.

content: RunArchive ownership: the long-lived owner (AgentOrchestrator, harness evaluator, CLI) constructs the archive and is responsible for calling close(). Transient adapter export() paths constructing RunArchive(run_dir.parent) do NOT call close() and rely on the refcount registry — the owning archive's close() releases the OS lock. AgentOrchestrator.aclose() now closes self._archive after the tool_backend.

AgentBackend.usage() is a non-abstract async method on the ABC with a Usage()-zero default (interfaces.py:138-148). Backends that cannot expose token counts inherit the default and surface zeros on RunResult.usage; the orchestrator reads RunResult.usage as the operator-visible signal of 'this backend cannot enforce cost ceilings'. Mirrors the same pattern as ToolBackend.aclose() (default no-op) — contract on the ABC, optional per-backend override. ADR-0028 explicitly leaves the abstract-vs-default trade-off to the implementer; default-with-zero was chosen to keep existing backends source-compatible without losing ADR-0028's 'fall open' visibility.

name: priced-vs-unpriced-backend-flag
description: When accumulating optional pricing data across calls (Claude SDK backend.py:100-117), use a separate boolean flag (_priced) to distinguish 'we received priced data and totals to 0.0' from 'we received no priced data ever'. Without the flag, the running float (_usd=0.0) is ambiguous — RunBudgetTracker would falsely pass the budget check on a never-priced backend. usage() returns usd=self._usd if self._priced else None so consumers see the 'fall open' signal explicitly, matching ADR-0028's 'Pricing the run is the backend's problem' consequence (§Negative #4).

content: ToolBackend.call_tool ABC takes a keyword-only 'capabilities: frozenset[str] | None = None' param. Backends that surface mutator tools (currently MCPToolBackend) must reject mutator tool calls whose tool_id is not in capabilities. None is treated identically to an empty frozenset (fail-closed). Observer tools always callable regardless of capabilities. Per ADR-0016 §3.2.

name: secretstr-at-the-config-boundary
description: Vendor secrets in AgentConfig (MCPServerSpec.env/headers, ClaudeBackendConfig.env) are typed as dict[str, SecretStr]. Plain-string YAML/JSON values coerce automatically (Pydantic for the BaseModel side, __post_init__ for the dataclass side). model_dump_json/repr/str all emit '**********' by default; cleartext only materialises at the SDK boundary via .get_secret_value() inside _build_stdio_env / _build_subprocess_env / _unwrap_headers. Composes with ADR-0029: SecretStr is the redaction layer; ADR-0029 is the env-passing policy. Both must hold.

content: When a secret-redaction test asserts 'cleartext not in repr/str', the secret value must contain a unique sentinel substring (e.g. 'do_not_leak') rather than a real-looking token whose substrings overlap with SecretStr's '**********' shape or with class names like 'Bearer' / 'SecretStr'. Otherwise the assertion can be a tautology and silently pass even if redaction is broken.

name: audit-event-hash-chain-sink-owned
description: AuditSink owns prev_event_sha256 chaining; producers (MCPToolBackend, future AgentBackend) emit standalone events and the sink wires the chain link via emit() returning a new event with prev set to the previous event's canonical_hash. Lets multiple producers share one totally-ordered chain by sharing one sink, instead of forking per-backend chains. canonical_hash() = sha256(stable_json(model_dump_json(by_alias=True))) so the chain link is reproducible across processes and after JSON round-trip.

title: Audit sink failures propagate (fail-closed)
rationale: If sink.emit raises, MCPToolBackend.call_tool propagates the exception rather than returning success. An unrecorded tool call cannot appear to succeed because the audit chain is the integrity record — a gap in the chain is a security failure, not a telemetry bug. Default audit_sink=None is a no-op so existing callers see no behavior change; opting into audit means accepting the fail-closed posture. Aligns with ADR-0029 default-deny-across-boundaries posture.

content: Archive checkpoints use write-then-rename atomicity via RunArchive._atomic_write(path, data): write to <path>.tmp then os.replace(tmp, path). Atomic on POSIX and Windows within the same filesystem, so a reader sees either the previous file or the new one — never a half-written one. Introduced for iteration_checkpoint.json (ADR-0036 §Consequence #4); VIGOR-c09b will retroactively migrate every archive.py write_text(...) site onto this helper for kill-9 safety across the full archive surface.
